doctor_turn: Hello, I’m Dr. Lee. What brought you in today?
patient_turn: Hi, um, I’ve been having this persistent dry cough for about three days now, and it’s getting worse. It’s really disrupting my sleep, and I’m kinda worried about it. I also felt hot today, and I’ve been really fatigued… and I had some chills and night sweats last night.

doctor_turn: When did the cough first start?
patient_turn: Uh, it started about three days ago. I mean, it was just a little annoying at first, but it’s gotten progressively worse since then.

doctor_turn: Where exactly do you feel the cough in your chest, or does it feel like it’s spreading anywhere?
patient_turn: Um, it’s mostly just a dry cough, you know? I don’t really feel any chest pain or anything like that, and it doesn’t feel like it’s spreading. It’s just… really persistent and annoying.

doctor_turn: What does the cough feel like? Is it more tickly, scratchy, or something else?
patient_turn: I’d say it feels more tickly, um, like there’s something irritating my throat. It’s not really scratchy, but it’s definitely persistent and makes me want to cough a lot, which is, you know, really frustrating.

doctor_turn: What have you noticed that makes the cough better or worse?
patient_turn: Honestly, I haven’t really found anything that makes it better. It just seems to get worse, especially at night when I’m trying to sleep. Um, I guess it might feel a little better when I drink warm liquids, but it doesn’t last long.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the cough starting?
patient_turn: Oh, I think it started a couple of days ago, or maybe it was just yesterday. But I definitely haven't had it for that long, like maybe a week or so.

doctor_turn: Can you describe where the cough feels like it’s coming from?
patient_turn: Um, it feels like it’s coming from my throat, you know? It’s not really in my chest or anything, just… kind of that tickly feeling in my throat that makes me cough.

doctor_turn: How would you describe the cough? Is it dry or does it produce any mucus?
patient_turn: It’s definitely dry, um, no mucus or anything like that. Just a persistent dry cough that’s really bothering me, especially at night.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the cough starting?
patient_turn: Uh, I first noticed it about three days ago. It started off kinda mild, but it’s just gotten worse since then.

doctor_turn: How has the cough changed since it first started?
patient_turn: Well, it’s definitely gotten worse since it first started. At first, it was just a little annoying, but now it’s really persistent and, um, it’s keeping me up at night. I’m kinda worried about it, to be honest.

doctor_turn: Can you describe where you feel the cough or if it feels like it's coming from your chest or throat?
patient_turn: It feels like it’s coming from my throat, um, not really my chest. It’s that tickly feeling that makes me want to cough a lot, but it’s definitely more in my throat than anywhere else.

doctor_turn: What does the cough feel like—dry, scratchy, or something else?
patient_turn: It’s mostly dry, um, and a bit tickly. I wouldn’t say it’s scratchy, but it definitely feels irritating, you know? Just really persistent and annoying.

doctor_turn: When did the cough first start, and has it changed since then?
patient_turn: I think it started about three days ago, but I’m not really sure. It might have been longer, like maybe a week, but it definitely feels like it's been around for just a couple of days.

doctor_turn: Have you noticed if the cough gets worse at any specific time of day?
patient_turn: Yeah, it definitely gets worse at night when I’m trying to sleep. I mean, it’s really disruptive, and I just can’t seem to get comfortable. It’s, um, really frustrating.

doctor_turn: Can you describe where you feel the cough or any discomfort in your chest?
patient_turn: Um, I don’t really feel any discomfort in my chest. It’s mostly just in my throat, you know? That tickly feeling that makes me cough a lot, but nothing painful or anything like that in my chest.

doctor_turn: Have you experienced any headaches along with your cough?
patient_turn: Yeah, I’ve had a mild headache, um, I think it’s probably related to all the coughing. It’s not too severe, but it’s definitely there and adds to how I’m feeling overall.

doctor_turn: Did you have any cold-like symptoms, like a runny nose or sore throat, before the cough started?
patient_turn: No, I didn’t have any cold-like symptoms before this started. It just kind of came out of nowhere, which is, um, a bit strange for me. I haven’t had a runny nose or sore throat or anything like that.

doctor_turn: Have you noticed any weight loss recently?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t noticed any weight loss. I mean, I feel a bit fatigued and, um, not great, but my weight seems to be the same.

doctor_turn: Do you have any past medical conditions or ongoing health issues?
patient_turn: Yeah, I have hypothyroidism, but it’s well controlled with Synthroid. My last TSH was normal about six months ago, so, um, I think that’s all good. Other than that, I don’t have any ongoing health issues.

doctor_turn: Have you had any surgeries in the past?
patient_turn: Yes, I had an appendectomy when I was a teenager, but it went fine, no complications or anything like that. That’s the only surgery I’ve had.

doctor_turn: What medications are you currently taking, including any over-the-counter or supplements?
patient_turn: I take a daily multivitamin, and, um, I’m on Synthroid for my hypothyroidism. I don’t take any other medications or over-the-counter stuff right now.

doctor_turn: Can you describe your living situation and who you live with?
patient_turn: Sure, I live in a townhouse with my husband and our two teenage kids. It’s, um, been a bit hectic lately because my husband has been acutely ill with vomiting and fever-like symptoms for the past couple of days. So, you know, it’s a bit concerning with everything going on.

doctor_turn: Do you have any family history of heart disease or other significant medical conditions?
patient_turn: Yeah, my sister recently had a heart attack, which is, um, really worrying. My parents both passed away from old age without any known heart disease or cancer, so that’s a relief, but my sister’s situation has me a bit anxious.

doctor_turn: Have you had any concerns about COVID-19 or experienced any symptoms related to it?
patient_turn: Yeah, I’ve been kinda worried about COVID-19, especially with my husband being sick and my symptoms. I asked about it because of the cough and everything, and I know they’re planning to do some tests to check for it and other infections.
